summary: Merged PR that, according to its title, split out additional character-specific item code/data. The available slice lacks the full diff because GitHub rejected it as too large, but PR metadata records 49 changed files with 34192 additions and 33371 deletions. The only concrete code evidence is a review hunk in src/melee/it/it_803F3100.c around the ItemLogicTable it_803F3100[118], where an entry was annotated as the Sheik chain item and review feedback fixed the comment spacing/name to `// Sheik chain`.

key_files.evidence: Review comment hunk shows an ItemLogicTable entry changed from a bare `{` to `{ // Sheik  chain`, with suggested correction to `{ // Sheik chain`.

what_changed: High-level PR title states that more character-specific items were split out.
PR metadata indicates this was a large change: 49 files changed, 34192 additions, and 33371 deletions.
In src/melee/it/it_803F3100.c, an entry in `struct ItemLogicTable it_803F3100[118]` was annotated as the Sheik chain item.
The full file list and diff content are not available in this slice; diff retrieval failed because the PR diff exceeded GitHub's 20000-line limit.
smart_moves: Treating character-specific items as separable units reduces pressure on large central item files and makes future item work easier to localize.
Adding inline identity comments to dense item logic tables helps reviewers and future decompilers map anonymous table entries to gameplay items before all symbols are fully named.
The review process caught a tiny but searchable formatting issue in an item-name comment, preserving consistent terminology for later grep-based navigation.
decomp_lessons: When splitting character-specific item code, leave clear breadcrumbs in central dispatch/logic tables so the split-out files can still be traced back to their item table entries.
Large mechanical split PRs should be made searchable through table comments, file names, and consistent item names because the raw diff may be too large for common review/export tools.
For dense arrays like `ItemLogicTable`, even small inline comments are valuable documentation if entries are otherwise still using generated names such as `it_803F3100`.
Avoid over-interpreting this PR without the full diff: the slice proves the PR touched the item logic table and Sheik chain annotation, but does not expose the exact split target files.

naming_conventions.convention: Use the character and item name in plain English comments for table entries.
naming_conventions.evidence: Reviewer suggestion changed `// Sheik  chain` to `// Sheik chain`.

naming_conventions.convention: Use single spacing in item identity comments.
naming_conventions.example: `{ // Sheik chain` rather than `{ // Sheik  chain`
naming_conventions.evidence: The only review comment was a spacing correction.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic is directly evidenced in the available PR slice.
The large addition/deletion counts are consistent with a major source organization change, but the slice does not show function bodies, asm diffs, or matching-specific review notes.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested correcting the inline table comment from `// Sheik  chain` to `// Sheik chain`.

follow_up_queries: Find merge commit 7ef38b3e6fc31bcdc812099024c583ef3582cda6 and inspect the 49 changed files for the exact character-specific item split pattern.
Search the repository history for `Sheik chain` and `it_803F3100[118]` to identify the final table entry and any split-out Sheik item source/header files.
Compare PR #938 against neighboring item split PRs to recover naming and file layout conventions for character-specific item modules.
